title: Festive Crispy Jamaican Cheonggukjang Onigiri
description: Salty, crispy onigiri shaped with a smoky cheonggukjang glaze, finished on a robata grill for a festive Jamaican twist.
instructions: Rinse jasmine rice until water runs clear, then cook with 4 cups water until fluffy.
While rice cooks, heat a small saucepan over medium heat, add sesame oil and fold in cheonggukjang, brown sugar, soy sauce, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes; stir until sauce thickens.
Once rice is done, fold the sauce into the warm rice until evenly coated, then stir in sliced green onions.
Let the mixture sit for 5 minutes so flavors meld.
Wet your hands with water, shape the rice mixture into bite‑sized triangular onigiri, pressing firmly.
Preheat the robata grill to medium‑high and lightly oil the surface.
Place the onigiri on the grill, pressing down with a spatula; cook 2–3 minutes per side until a crisp, golden crust forms.
Flip carefully and grill the other side until evenly crispy.
Remove from grill and let cool on a rack for 1 minute.
Serve immediately with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and a side of lime wedges for a festive touch.
